title: rosa rubiginosa f. dolorosa
intro: Rosa rubiginosa f. dolorosa is a variant of the fragrant rose, also known as the sweetbriar or fragrant rose, which is a type of shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa f. dolorosa include relief from headaches, menstrual cramps, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
